aliases: Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme Nagaland

local_names.en: Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme Nagaland

exclusion_rules.description: Only Indigenous Inhabitants or Permanent Residents of Nagaland (and eligible state government employees/pensioners) are covered. A valid Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (IIC) or Permanent Resident Certificate (PRC) is required for the General category.

The General (GEN) category specifically targets Nagaland residents NOT already covered under AB PM-JAY. Households already covered by PM-JAY use that scheme; CMHIS-GEN fills the gap for those left out.

family_criteria.family_definition: Beneficiary and dependents as declared at enrollment, identified by a Household ID. For the Employee & Pensioner (EP) category, this covers the government employee/pensioner and their declared dependents. For the General (GEN) category, it covers the indigenous/resident household. Coverage is a family floater.

covered_procedures: Cashless hospitalization at empanelled government and private hospitals (within and outside Nagaland)
Orthopedics (joint replacement, fracture and spine surgery)
Pre- and post-hospitalization expenses as per scheme package rules
Higher Rs 20 lakh annual cover for the Employee & Pensioner (EP) category
excluded_procedures: Treatment costs above the applicable annual family ceiling

required_documents.name: Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (IIC) or Permanent Resident Certificate (PRC)
required_documents.local_name_hi: स्वदेशी निवासी प्रमाणपत्र (IIC) या स्थायी निवासी प्रमाणपत्र (PRC)
required_documents.notes: Required for the General (GEN) category; the IIC/PRC must be issued after 2016. Government employees use their PIMS code instead.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Identity and e-KYC proof for the beneficiary and dependents.

required_documents.name: PIMS Code (government employees) / PPO Book (pensioners)
required_documents.notes: Required for the Employee & Pensioner (EP) category. PIMS code for serving employees; Pension Payment Order (PPO) book for pensioners.

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and Household ID communications.

required_documents.name: Dependent Supporting Documents
required_documents.notes: Proof of dependency for spouse, children, and dependent parents.

enrollment_channels: Primary Health Centres (PHC) and Community Health Centres (CHC)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Determine your category — Employee & Pensioner (EP) for government staff/pensioners, or General (GEN) for indigenous residents not covered by PM-JAY
Step 2: Gather documents — IIC/PRC (GEN) or PIMS code / PPO book (EP), plus Aadhaar and mobile number
Step 3: Submit documents at a PHC, CHC, Treasury Office, or designated hospital (or apply on cmhis.nagaland.gov.in)
Step 4: Details are verified by the implementing authority
Step 5: On approval, a Household ID is generated for the family
Step 6: Use the Household ID at any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment up to the applicable annual cover (Rs 5 lakh GEN / Rs 20 lakh EP)
description_for_embedding: The Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS) is Nagaland's flagship state health insurance programme, providing cashless treatment at empanelled government and private hospitals within and outside the state. It operates in two categories. CMHIS (EP) — the Employee & Pensioner category — gives Rs 20 lakh per family per year to regular state government employees, pensioners receiving a medical allowance, legislators, public-sector workers, and their dependents. CMHIS (GEN), the General category, gives Rs 5 lakh per family per year to Indigenous Inhabitants or Permanent Residents of Nagaland who are NOT covered under Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY, as well as state contractual and ad-hoc workers, accredited journalists, and households without other coverage. Eligibility for the General category requires a valid Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (IIC) or Permanent Resident Certificate (PRC) issued after 2016, plus Aadhaar; employees and pensioners enroll using their PIMS code or PPO book respectively. There is no income test — the scheme is designed to give universal coverage to bona fide residents not otherwise insured. Enrollment is done at Primary Health Centres, Community Health Centres, Treasury Offices, designated hospitals, or the online portal, after which a Household ID is issued. The scheme covers hospitalization for surgery, cardiology, oncology, orthopedics, dialysis, neurology, maternity, and critical illnesses. Helpline 1800-202-3380; portal cmhis.nagaland.gov.in.
